content.text: CI shouldn't start integration tests when unit tests fail. Probably they are started in parallel now. But integration tests take very long. Better to wait for successful lint and unit tests.
I'll look at the CI workflow configuration to understand how the jobs are currently wired.

9	    name: Check if lint/tests needed
10	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
11	    outputs:
12	      should-run: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.code }}
13	    steps:

16	        id: filter

23	              - 'tests/contract/**'

57	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

66	      - name: Run unit and contract tests

70	    name: CI Status

73	    if: always()

79	            exit 0

82	            exit 0

1	name: Integration Tests
2	
3	on:
4	  pull_request:
5	    branches:
6	      - main
7	    types:
8	      - opened
9	      - synchronize
10	      - reopened
11	      - ready_for_review
12	  workflow_dispatch:
13	
14	jobs:
15	  check-changes:
16	    name: Check if integration tests needed
17	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
18	    outputs:
19	      should-run: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.integration }}
20	    steps:
21	      - uses: actions/checkout@v7
22	      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@v4
23	        id: filter
24	        with:
25	          filters: |
26	            integration:
27	              - '.github/workflows/integration-tests.yml'
28	              - 'src/**'
29	              - 'tests/run-integration-tests.sh'
30	              - 'tests/integration/**/!(upgrade-vms.sh)'
31	              - 'install.sh'
32	              - 'pyproject.toml'
33	              - 'uv.lock'
34	
35	  integration:
36	    name: Integration Tests
37	    needs: check-changes
38	    if: github.event.pull_request.draft == false && needs.check-changes.outputs.should-run == 'true'
39	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
40	    concurrency:
41	      group: pc-switcher-integration
42	      cancel-in-progress: false
43	    steps:
44	      - uses: actions/checkout@v7
45	        with:
46	          fetch-depth: 0
47	
48	      - name: Install uv
49	        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v8.3.2
50	
51	      - name: Check secrets
52	        run: |
53	          if [[ -z "${{ secrets.HCLOUD_TOKEN }}" ]] || [[ -z "${{ secrets.HETZNER_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY }}" ]]; then
54	            echo "::error::Integration tests cannot run: Required secrets (HCLOUD_TOKEN, HETZNER_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY) are not available"
55	            exit 1
56	          fi
57	
58	      - name: Install Hetzner Cloud CLI
59	        uses: hetznercloud/setup-hcloud@v2
60	
61	      - name: Setup SSH key and get existing VM IPs
62	        id: vm_ips
63	        run: |
64	          mkdir -p ~/.ssh
65	          echo "${{ secrets.HETZNER_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY }}" > ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
66	          echo "${{ secrets.SSH_AUTHORIZED_KEY_CI }}" > ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
67	          chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
68	          # Get IPs once (if VMs exist) for known_hosts and later use
69	          PC1_IP=$(hcloud server ip pc1 2>/dev/null || echo "")
70	          PC2_IP=$(hcloud server ip pc2 2>/dev/null || echo "")
71	          if [[ -n "$PC1_IP" ]]; then
72	            ssh-keyscan -H "$PC1_IP" >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts 2>/dev/null || true
73	            echo "pc1_ip=$PC1_IP" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
74	          fi
75	          if [[ -n "$PC2_IP" ]]; then
76	            ssh-keyscan -H "$PC2_IP" >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts 2>/dev/null || true
77	            echo "pc2_ip=$PC2_IP" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
78	          fi
79	        env:
80	          HCLOUD_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HCLOUD_TOKEN }}
81	
82	      - name: Provision test infrastructure
83	        id: provision
84	        env:
85	          SECRETS_JSON: ${{ toJSON(secrets) }}
86	          HCLOUD_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HCLOUD_TOKEN }}
87	          # Long-lived read-only PAT baked into the VM at provision time, so
88	          # GitHub API calls keep working after this job ends (unlike the
89	          # ephemeral Actions GITHUB_TOKEN). See issue #163.
90	          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GH_API_TOKEN_RO }}
91	        run: |
92	          set -o pipefail
93	          # Collect all SSH_AUTHORIZED_KEY_* secrets into single variable
94	          SSH_AUTHORIZED_KEYS=$(echo "$SECRETS_JSON" | jq -r 'to_entries[] | select(.key | startswith("SSH_AUTHORIZED_KEY_")) | .value')
95	          export SSH_AUTHORIZED_KEYS
96	          tests/integration/scripts/provision-test-infra.sh 2>&1 | tee provision-output.log
97	          # If VMs were just created, get their IPs now
98	          if [[ -z "${{ steps.vm_ips.outputs.pc1_ip }}" ]]; then
99	            PC1_IP=$(hcloud server ip pc1)
100	            echo "pc1_ip=$PC1_IP" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
101	          fi
102	          if [[ -z "${{ steps.vm_ips.outputs.pc2_ip }}" ]]; then
103	            PC2_IP=$(hcloud server ip pc2)
104	            echo "pc2_ip=$PC2_IP" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
105	          fi
106	        timeout-minutes: 30
107	
108	      - name: Run integration tests
109	        run: |
110	          set -o pipefail
111	          ./tests/run-integration-tests.sh 2>&1 | tee pytest-output.log
112	        env:
113	          HCLOUD_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HCLOUD_TOKEN }}
114	          PC_SWITCHER_TEST_PC1_HOST: ${{ steps.vm_ips.outputs.pc1_ip || steps.provision.outputs.pc1_ip }}
115	          PC_SWITCHER_TEST_PC2_HOST: ${{ steps.vm_ips.outputs.pc2_ip || steps.provision.outputs.pc2_ip }}
116	          PC_SWITCHER_TEST_USER: testuser
117	          CI_JOB_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
118	          # Long-lived read-only PAT, not the ephemeral Actions token, so reused
119	          # or pre-provisioned VMs (conftest injection path) stay authenticated.
120	          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GH_API_TOKEN_RO }}
121	        timeout-minutes: 30
122	
123	      - name: Upload test logs
124	        if: always()
125	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
126	        with:
127	          name: test-logs
128	          path: |
129	            provision-output.log
130	            pytest-output.log
131	          retention-days: 14
132	          if-no-files-found: warn
133	
134	  status:
135	    name: Integration Tests Status
136	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
137	    needs: [check-changes, integration]
138	    if: always()
139	    steps:
140	      - name: Report status
141	        run: |
142	          if [[ "${{ github.event.pull_request.draft }}" == "true" ]]; then
143	            echo "✓ Integration tests skipped (draft PR)"
144	            exit 0
145	          elif [[ "${{ needs.check-changes.outputs.should-run }}" != "true" ]]; then
146	            echo "✓ Integration tests skipped (no relevant changes)"
147	            exit 0
148	          elif [[ "${{ needs.integration.result }}" == "success" ]]; then
149	            echo "✓ Integration tests passed"
150	            exit 0
151	          else
152	            echo "✗ Integration tests failed or were cancelled"
153	            exit 1
154	          fi

content.text:
Both are **separate workflows on different triggers**: `ci.yml` runs lint + unit on `push` (every branch), `integration-tests.yml` runs on `pull_request`. On a PR push both fire simultaneously, so integration doesn't wait for lint/unit — exactly what you're seeing.

There's no cross-workflow `needs:`. Let me confirm the cleanest gating action's current version before proposing.

input.query: lewagon wait-on-check-action latest version 2025 github action wait for check same commit

result.output:
Web search results for query: "lewagon wait-on-check-action latest version 2025 github action wait for check same commit"

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - lewagon/wait-on-check-action: Pause until a job in another workflow completes successfully. · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/lewagon/wait-on-check-action"},{"title":"Releases · lewagon/wait-on-check-action","url":"https://github.com/lewagon/wait-on-check-action/releases"},{"title":"Issues · lewagon/wait-on-check-action","url":"https://github.com/lewagon/wait-on-check-action/issues"},{"title":"Wait on check · Actions · GitHub Marketplace · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/marketplace/actions/wait-on-check"},{"title":"wait-on-check-action/action.yml at master · lewagon/wait-on-check-action","url":"https://github.com/lewagon/wait-on-check-action/blob/master/action.yml"},{"title":"Le Wagon · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/LeWagon"},{"title":"File Finder · lewagon/wait-on-check-action","url":"https://github.com/lewagon/wait-on-check-action/find/master"},{"title":"Wait using allowed_conclusions · Workflow runs · lewagon/wait-on-check-action","url":"https://github.com/lewagon/wait-on-check-action/actions/workflows/wait-for-allowed-conclusions.yml"},{"title":"doesn't work on pull requests · Issue #25 · lewagon/wait-on-check-action","url":"https://github.com/lewagon/wait-on-check-action/issues/25"},{"title":"`lewagon/wait-on-check-action` fails to run due since Bundler `2.5.0` dropped support for Ruby 2.7. · Issue #98 · lewagon/wait-on-check-action","url":"https://github.com/lewagon/wait-on-check-action/issues/98"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the lewagon wait-on-check-action:

## Latest Version

The latest version shown in the documentation is v1.8.1, which is used in the current examples on both the GitHub repository and GitHub Marketplace.

## Purpose

This action can be used to halt any workflow until required checks for a given ref (e.g., in a sibling workflow) pass successfully.

## Key Features for Waiting on Same Commit

The action supports several parameters that allow you to wait for checks on the same commit:

- **ref parameter**: You can use `ref: ${{ github.sha }}` to wait for checks on a specific commit SHA
- **check-name**: Specify the exact name of the check to wait for
- **check-regexp**: Use a regular expression pattern to match multiple checks
- **running-workflow-name**: Wait for a specific workflow by name

The action polls the GitHub API to wait for checks to complete successfully on the specified commit reference, making it useful for coordinating between different workflows or jobs that can't use the native `needs` keyword.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text:
Confirmed: latest is **v1.8.1** ([releases](https://github.com/lewagon/wait-on-check-action/releases)).

Here's the situation and my recommended fix.

## Why it happens
`ci.yml` (lint + unit) triggers on `push`; `integration-tests.yml` triggers on `pull_request`. On a PR push both fire at once. GitHub Actions has no cross-workflow `needs:`, so integration can't natively depend on the CI job.

## Approach
Add a gate job to `integration-tests.yml` that blocks on CI's aggregate **"CI Status"** check for the same commit, and make `integration` depend on it. I'll wait on `CI Status` (not `Lint`/`Unit Tests` individually) because it always runs — when lint/unit are skipped via path-filter, the individual checks don't exist and waiting by name would hang, whereas `CI Status` reports success for the skipped case.

Key detail: use `github.event.pull_request.head.sha`, not `github.sha` (the PR merge commit) — CI's checks attach to the head commit ([issue #25](https://github.com/lewagon/wait-on-check-action/issues/25)).

If CI Status fails, the gate job fails and `integration` is skipped — no wasted long-running VM job.

input.old_string:
integration:
    name: Integration Tests
    needs: check-changes
    if: github.event.pull_request.draft == false && needs.check-changes.outputs.should-run == 'true'
input.new_string:
wait-for-ci:
    name: Wait for lint and unit tests
    needs: check-changes
    if: github.event.pull_request.draft == false && needs.check-changes.outputs.should-run == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # No native cross-workflow `needs:`. CI (lint + unit) runs in ci.yml on the
      # push event; block on its aggregate "CI Status" check for this commit so
      # the long-running integration job never starts on a red build.
      # Must use the PR head SHA, not github.sha (the merge commit) — ci.yml's
      # checks attach to the head commit. "CI Status" always runs (even when
      # lint/unit are path-filtered out), so it is safe to wait on by name.
      - name: Wait for CI Status
        uses: lewagon/wait-on-check-action@v1.8.1
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          check-name: CI Status
          repo-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          wait-interval: 15

  integration:
    name: Integration Tests
    needs: [check-changes, wait-for-ci]
    if: github.event.pull_request.draft == false && needs.check-changes.outputs.should-run == 'true'

content.text: Now update the `status` job so its message stays accurate when integration is skipped because CI was red.

input.old_string:
status:
    name: Integration Tests Status
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [check-changes, integration]
    if: always()
    steps:
      - name: Report status
        run: |
          if [[ "${{ github.event.pull_request.draft }}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "✓ Integration tests skipped (draft PR)"
            exit 0
          elif [[ "${{ needs.check-changes.outputs.should-run }}" != "true" ]]; then
            echo "✓ Integration tests skipped (no relevant changes)"
            exit 0
          elif [[ "${{ needs.integration.result }}" == "success" ]]; then
            echo "✓ Integration tests passed"
            exit 0
          else
            echo "✗ Integration tests failed or were cancelled"
            exit 1
          fi
input.new_string:
status:
    name: Integration Tests Status
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [check-changes, wait-for-ci, integration]
    if: always()
    steps:
      - name: Report status
        run: |
          if [[ "${{ github.event.pull_request.draft }}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "✓ Integration tests skipped (draft PR)"
            exit 0
          elif [[ "${{ needs.check-changes.outputs.should-run }}" != "true" ]]; then
            echo "✓ Integration tests skipped (no relevant changes)"
            exit 0
          elif [[ "${{ needs.wait-for-ci.result }}" != "success" ]]; then
            echo "✗ Integration tests blocked: lint/unit tests did not pass"
            exit 1
          elif [[ "${{ needs.integration.result }}" == "success" ]]; then
            echo "✓ Integration tests passed"
            exit 0
          else
            echo "✗ Integration tests failed or were cancelled"
            exit 1
          fi

content.text: Two docs describe this: ADR-008 and `docs/ops/testing-architecture.md`. Let me read the relevant sections.

result.output:
1	# ADR-008: CI Pipeline with Draft-Aware Integration Tests
2	
3	Status: Accepted
4	Date: 2025-12-28
5	
6	## TL;DR
7	Lint and Unit Tests run on all branches and PR-triggered integration tests that skip draft PRs while ensuring all tests pass before merge to main.
8	
9	## Implementation Rules
10	- Integration tests trigger on `pull_request` events
11	- Skip integration tests on draft PRs (`if: github.event.pull_request.draft == false`)
12	- Branch protection requires all three checks: Lint, Unit Tests, Integration Tests
13	
14	## Context
15	Integration tests are slow (5-30 min) and use shared VM infrastructure. Originally configured with GitHub merge queues, but this created a chicken-and-egg problem: tests only ran in the merge queue, but PRs couldn't enter the queue without passing tests.
16	
17	## Decision
18	- **Fast checks on every push**: Lint and Unit Tests run on all branches
19	- **Integration tests on ready PRs only**: Triggered by `pull_request` events with draft check
20	- **Branch protection gates merge**: All three checks required before merge
21	- **No merge queue**: Simpler workflow without merge queue complexity
22	
23	## Consequences
24	
25	**Positive**:
26	- Fast iteration on draft PRs (no waiting for integration tests)
27	- Integration tests run automatically when PR marked ready
28	- Simple, understandable CI flow
29	- No merge queue configuration to maintain
30	
31	**Negative**:
32	- Integration tests may run multiple times if commits pushed after ready
33	- Concurrent PRs compete for shared VM infrastructure (mitigated by concurrency group)
34	
35	## References
36	- [CI Configuration Details](../ops/ci-setup.md)
37	- [Testing Guide](../dev/testing-guide.md)
38	- [ADR-006: VM-Based Testing Framework](adr-006-testing-framework.md)
39

270	## CI/CD Integration
271	
272	### GitHub Actions Workflow Structure
273	
274	The repository uses three GitHub Actions workflows:
275	
276	| Workflow | File | Triggers | Purpose |
277	|----------|------|----------|---------|
278	| CI | `ci.yml` | Every push | Lint (basedpyright, ruff, codespell) and unit tests |
279	| Integration Tests | `integration-tests.yml` | PR ready for review | Full integration tests on Hetzner VMs |
280	| VM Updates | `vm-updates.yml` | Daily (2am UTC) | Keep test VMs updated with OS patches |
281	
282	### Path Filtering
283	
284	Both CI and integration tests use path filtering (`dorny/paths-filter`) to skip checks when only unrelated files change:
285	
286	**Integration tests run when**:
287	- `.github/workflows/integration-tests.yml`
288	- `src/**`
289	- `tests/integration/**`
290	- `install.sh`
291	- `pyproject.toml`
292	- `uv.lock`
293	
294	Documentation-only changes skip integration tests, allowing quick merges.
295	
296	### Integration Test Trigger Strategy
297	
298	Integration tests are expensive (they use cloud VMs), so they don't run on every commit:
299	
300	| PR State | Integration Tests |
301	|----------|-------------------|
302	| Draft PR | **Skipped** |
303	| Marked "Ready for review" | **Runs** |
304	| New commits to ready PR | **Runs** |
305	| Manual trigger | **Runs** (via `workflow_dispatch`) |
306	
307	### Concurrency Control
308	
309	Integration tests use GitHub Actions `concurrency.group` to prevent parallel runs:
310	
311	```yaml
312	concurrency:
313	  group: pc-switcher-integration
314	  cancel-in-progress: false
315	```
316	
317	### Required Secrets
318	
319	| Secret | Description |
320	|--------|-------------|
321	| `HCLOUD_TOKEN` | Hetzner Cloud API token |
322	| `HETZNER_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY` | SSH private key for CI VM access |
323	| `SSH_AUTHORIZED_KEY_CI` | CI public key for VM access |
324	| `SSH_AUTHORIZED_KEY_*` | Developer public keys (one per developer/machine) |
325	
326	## Environment Variables and Configuration
327	
328	### Test Configuration
329

adr-001-adr.md

1	# ADR-001: Use Architecture Decision Records (ADR)
2	
3	Status: Accepted  
4	Date: 2025-11-13
5	
6	## TL;DR
7	Use immutable Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) to document key architectural decisions.
8	
9	## Implementation Rules
10	- Follow the folder structure below.
11	- Keep `_index.md` up to date as a list with all and only current ADRs.
12	- Each ADR must follow the structure below.
13	- Avoid duplicating information across ADR sections; only repeat in TL;DR. Repeat in other sections only if necessary for clarity.
14	- Keep ADRs concise. Place background and considerations in `docs/adr/considerations/` documents.
15	- ADRs are immutable: they can be superseded but not edited after acceptance.
16	- Add two-way references between superseded and current ADRs.
17	
18	### Folder structure
19	
20	```plain
21	docs/adr/
22	├── considerations/                      -- Documents with additional context and considerations for ADRs
23	├── _index.md                            -- Summary of all current ADRs
24	├── adr-001-adr.md                       -- This ADR
25	├── adr-002-postgresql-timescaledb.md    -- ADR-002
26	└── ...
27	```
28	
29	### Document structure
30	
31	```markdown
32	# ADR-XXX: [Title]
33	
34	Status: [Draft|Accepted|Superseded by ADR-XXX|Deprecated]
35	Date: YYYY-MM-DD
36	Supersedes: ADR-XXX (if applicable)
37	
38	## TL;DR
39	One sentence decision summary for quick AI parsing.
40	
41	## Implementation Rules
42	- Specific technical constraints
43	- Required patterns/approaches
44	- Forbidden approaches
45	
46	## Context
47	[Brief context - keep under 200 words]
48	
49	## Decision
50	[What was decided - bullet points preferred]
51	
52	## Consequences
53	**Positive**: Critical implementation requirements (bullet points)
54	**Negative**: Known anti-patterns from this decision (bullet points)
55	
56	## References
57	- Links to relevant discussions, chats, documents or websites
58	```
59	
60	## Context
61	ADRs (Architecture Decision Records) are lightweight documents that capture important architectural decisions made during a project's development.
62	
63	## Decision
64	- Use ADRs to document key architectural decisions
65	- See above [implementation rules](#implementation-rules)
66	- In the main project repository, or in subrepos if only relevant for that context
67	
68	Example ADR: see [`002-postgresql-timescaledb.md`](docs/adr/002-postgresql-timescaledb.md).
69	
70	### AI agent integration strategy
71	- AI agent reads `_index.md` first (always in context)
72	- Loads specific ADRs only when relevant to current task
73	- Can search workspace files to find relevant ADRs
74	
75	## Granularity Guidelines
76	These guidelines help keep ADR maintenance overhead low while preserving meaningful decision boundaries for a solo project.
77	
78	Combine when decisions are:
79	- Tightly coupled – changing one necessitates changing the other
80	- Same abstraction level – e.g. both infrastructure OR both application-level
81	- Same timeline – decided together and likely to change together
82	
83	Split when decisions:
84	- Can evolve independently – different tools, lifecycles, or replacement likelihood
85	- Have different stakeholders (even if it is just different "hats" you wear)
86	- Cross major boundaries – infrastructure vs. application vs. business/domain logic
87	
88	### Anti-pattern: Over-Granularity
89	Avoid creating many tiny ADRs that add maintenance cost without clarity benefits. For example, DO NOT split like:
90	- ADR-004: Use Kubernetes
91	- ADR-005: Use K3s instead of K8s
92	- ADR-006: Use Hetzner Cloud
93	- ADR-007: Use Terraform
94	- ADR-008: Use kube-hetzner module
95	
96	Prefer a higher-level ADR that captures the cohesive deployment/infrastructure decision, unless individual elements are likely to change independently.
97	
98	## Consequences
99	**Positive:**
100	- Future self documentation - Remember why you chose PostgreSQL over MongoDB 6 months later
101	- Decision traceability - Understand the reasoning behind technology choices
102	- Avoid repeated debates - Don't re-argue the same decisions
103	- Rapid context switching and mistake learning
104	- Onboarding aid - If you ever bring someone else onto the project
105	
106	Specific benefits of immutability:
107	- Time travel - Understand why past-you made certain choices
108	- Context preservation - Original constraints and reasoning remain clear
109	- Rapid context switching - Quickly recall the "why" behind complex choices
110	- Mistake learning - Track which decisions worked out and which didn't
111	
112	**Negative:**
113	- Initial overhead of writing ADRs
114	- Requires discipline to keep them updated
115

content.text: `docs/adr/adr-008-ci-pipeline.md` is **immutable** (ADR-001 rule) and this change doesn't reverse it — it refines *ordering* while its decision ("all three checks required before merge") still holds. A superseding ADR for a sequencing tweak would be over-granular per ADR-001's own anti-pattern guidance. So I'll leave the ADR and update the living ops doc, `testing-architecture.md`, which documents the actual mechanics.

input.old_string:
| PR State | Integration Tests |
|----------|-------------------|
| Draft PR | **Skipped** |
| Marked "Ready for review" | **Runs** |
| New commits to ready PR | **Runs** |
| Manual trigger | **Runs** (via `workflow_dispatch`) |
input.new_string:
| PR State | Integration Tests |
|----------|-------------------|
| Draft PR | **Skipped** |
| Marked "Ready for review" | **Runs** |
| New commits to ready PR | **Runs** |
| Manual trigger | **Runs** (via `workflow_dispatch`) |

### Gating on Lint and Unit Tests

Integration tests are the most expensive checks, so they must not start until the fast checks pass. Lint and unit tests run in `ci.yml` on the `push` event, while integration tests run in `integration-tests.yml` on `pull_request`; GitHub Actions has no cross-workflow `needs:`, so on a PR push both workflows would otherwise start in parallel.

To gate them, `integration-tests.yml` has a `wait-for-ci` job that blocks on CI's aggregate `CI Status` check for the PR head commit (using [`lewagon/wait-on-check-action`](https://github.com/lewagon/wait-on-check-action)), and the `integration` job depends on it. If `CI Status` fails, `wait-for-ci` fails and the integration job is skipped — no VM infrastructure is provisioned on a red build.

Notes:
- It waits on the PR head SHA, not `github.sha` (the merge commit) — `ci.yml`'s checks attach to the head commit.
- It waits on `CI Status` (the aggregate job) rather than `Lint`/`Unit Tests` individually, because those are skipped by path filtering on some changes and would not exist as checks to wait on, whereas `CI Status` always reports.

content.text:
Done. Summary of what changed and why.

## Root cause
`ci.yml` (lint + unit) triggers on `push`; `integration-tests.yml` triggers on `pull_request`. On a PR push both workflows start at once — so integration wasn't waiting for lint/unit. GitHub Actions has no cross-workflow `needs:`.

## Fix — `.github/workflows/integration-tests.yml`
- Added a **`wait-for-ci`** job that blocks on CI's aggregate `CI Status` check for the PR head commit via [`lewagon/wait-on-check-action@v1.8.1`](https://github.com/lewagon/wait-on-check-action/releases) (latest).
- Made `integration` depend on it (`needs: [check-changes, wait-for-ci]`). Red lint/unit → `wait-for-ci` fails → integration is **skipped**, so no VMs are provisioned on a broken build.
- Updated the `status` job to report "blocked: lint/unit did not pass" instead of a misleading "failed/cancelled".

Two deliberate details: it waits on `github.event.pull_request.head.sha` (not the PR merge `github.sha`, where CI's checks don't attach — [issue #25](https://github.com/lewagon/wait-on-check-action/issues/25)), and on the always-present `CI Status` aggregate rather than `Lint`/`Unit Tests`, which path-filtering can skip out of existence.

## Docs updated
- `docs/ops/testing-architecture.md` — new "Gating on Lint and Unit Tests" section.
- `docs/ops/ci-setup.md` — added the `wait-for-ci` job to the job list and made the flow diagram show integration gating on lint+unit (was a hidden layout link).
- `docs/adr/adr-008-ci-pipeline.md` — **left untouched**: ADRs are immutable (ADR-001), and this refines ordering without reversing its decision; a superseding ADR for a sequencing tweak would hit ADR-001's own over-granularity anti-pattern.

Note: I set the `wait-for-ci` gate to also apply to draft PRs' skip logic via the same `if:` guard, so draft PRs still skip everything cleanly. This isn't pushed — review and commit when ready.

One thing to confirm on your side: the required branch-protection check is still `Integration Tests / Integration Tests Status`, which is unchanged, so no branch-protection update is needed.
update adr 008. I allow you to
Editing ADR-008 in place as authorized (adding the gating refinement).

## TL;DR
Lint and Unit Tests run on all branches and PR-triggered integration tests that skip draft PRs while ensuring all tests pass before merge to main.

## Implementation Rules
- Integration tests trigger on `pull_request` events
- Skip integration tests on draft PRs (`if: github.event.pull_request.draft == false`)
- Branch protection requires all three checks: Lint, Unit Tests, Integration Tests

## TL;DR
Lint and Unit Tests run on all branches; PR-triggered integration tests skip draft PRs and only start after lint and unit tests pass, ensuring all tests pass before merge to main.

## Implementation Rules
- Integration tests trigger on `pull_request` events
- Skip integration tests on draft PRs (`if: github.event.pull_request.draft == false`)
- Integration tests gate on lint + unit tests: a `wait-for-ci` job blocks on CI's `CI Status` check for the PR head commit (`lewagon/wait-on-check-action`) and `integration` needs it, so the expensive VM job never starts on a red build
- Branch protection requires all three checks: Lint, Unit Tests, Integration Tests

## Decision
- **Fast checks on every push**: Lint and Unit Tests run on all branches
- **Integration tests on ready PRs only**: Triggered by `pull_request` events with draft check
- **Branch protection gates merge**: All three checks required before merge
- **No merge queue**: Simpler workflow without merge queue complexity

- **Fast checks on every push**: Lint and Unit Tests run on all branches
- **Integration tests on ready PRs only**: Triggered by `pull_request` events with draft check
- **Integration gated on fast checks**: The integration job waits for CI's `CI Status` check to pass before running, so a red lint/unit build never provisions VM infrastructure. Waits on the PR head SHA (not the merge commit) and on the aggregate `CI Status` (not `Lint`/`Unit Tests`, which path filtering can skip out of existence)
- **Branch protection gates merge**: All three checks required before merge
- **No merge queue**: Simpler workflow without merge queue complexity

input.old_string:
**Positive**:
- Fast iteration on draft PRs (no waiting for integration tests)
- Integration tests run automatically when PR marked ready
- Simple, understandable CI flow
- No merge queue configuration to maintain

**Negative**:
- Integration tests may run multiple times if commits pushed after ready
- Concurrent PRs compete for shared VM infrastructure (mitigated by concurrency group)
input.new_string:
**Positive**:
- Fast iteration on draft PRs (no waiting for integration tests)
- Integration tests run automatically when PR marked ready
- No VM infrastructure wasted on builds that already fail lint or unit tests
- Simple, understandable CI flow
- No merge queue configuration to maintain

**Negative**:
- Integration tests may run multiple times if commits pushed after ready
- Concurrent PRs compete for shared VM infrastructure (mitigated by concurrency group)
- Cross-workflow gating relies on a third-party wait action rather than native `needs:` (lint/unit run on `push` in `ci.yml`, integration on `pull_request`)

ADR-008 is now updated: TL;DR, Implementation Rules, Decision, and Consequences all reflect the gating. Combined with the earlier changes:

- `.github/workflows/integration-tests.yml` — `wait-for-ci` gate + `integration` depends on it + accurate `status` messaging.
- `docs/adr/adr-008-ci-pipeline.md` — decision record updated.
- `docs/ops/testing-architecture.md` and `docs/ops/ci-setup.md` — mechanics + flow diagram updated.

ci: gate integration tests on lint + unit passing

Integration tests (pull_request) ran in parallel with lint/unit (push in
ci.yml) since Actions has no cross-workflow needs:, so expensive VM jobs
started even on red builds. Add a wait-for-ci job that blocks on CI's
aggregate "CI Status" check for the PR head commit
(lewagon/wait-on-check-action); integration now needs it and is skipped
when lint/unit fail. status job reports the blocked case distinctly.

Waits on the head SHA (not the merge commit, where ci.yml checks don't
attach) and on the aggregate CI Status (not Lint/Unit Tests, which path
filtering can skip out of existence).

Update ADR-008 and ops CI docs to match.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/REDACTED
EOF
